prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: Atom‐resolved Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor of formaldehyde; headline value reported here is the largest electrophilic Fukui index f⁺.
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` → B3LYP/def2-TZVP with exact four-center integrals (density fitting off by default) in PySCF.
Geometry provenance: fixed experimental geometry supplied by the user (file “/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz”); no re-optimization — single-point N, N ± 1 charge calculations (`B3LYP/def2-TZVP//input geometry`).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent model requested).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet parent (charge 0, multiplicity 1); cation and anion computed as doublets (charges ±1, multiplicity 2) per default Fukui protocol.
Software: PySCF (via chemkit fukui-reactivity skill).
Convergence & caveats: All three SCF calculations converged; integrity.status = "ok", integrity.trustworthy = true.  No engine warnings were reported (warnings array empty).  Integrity self-checks report sum(f⁺)=1.0000 and sum(f⁻)=1.0000 (as expected).

Key numerical results (Mulliken partitioning, atom order O(1) C(2) H(3) H(4)):
• f⁺ = [0.46813824, 0.70839130, −0.088269392, −0.088260145]
• f⁻ = [0.46742464, 0.11244422, 0.21007563, 0.21005551]
• Dual descriptor Δf = [0.00071359555, 0.59594709, −0.29834503, −0.29831566]
Most electrophilic site: atom 2 (C), f⁺ = 0.70839130 (reported as headline value).
Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (O), f⁻ = 0.46742464.

Reproducibility
Command (from cli_invocation):
/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 --stdout json /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpgvpzmxp_/live.json

The full result JSON is at “/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpgvpzmxp_/live.json”.  The live PySCF/chemkit stdout and SCF progress were streamed during the run to the automatically generated log file (name beginning with “fukui_” and timestamp) in the working directory.

No warnings reported.
